description_long: This sculpture depicts a robed male figure with a full beard and curly hair, standing upright on a rectangular base. The figure holds a long staff or rod in his left hand and an open book or tablet in his right, suggesting a scholarly or religious theme. The drapery of the robe is intricately carved, with deep folds and a sense of movement, and the facial features are expressive and detailed. The back of the sculpture reveals a hollowed-out interior with visible wooden support structures, indicating it is not solid throughout. The surface has a uniform, warm brown color, typical of aged or stained wood. The overall craftsmanship suggests a traditional, possibly historical or religious, subject matter.
description: A detailed wooden sculpture of a bearded robed man holding a staff and an open book, with expressive features and intricate drapery.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full figure, facial details, and both the staff and open book clearly visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the robe's folds and the profile of the face, with the staff and book in partial silhouette.
description_view_2: Rear view exposing the hollowed interior and internal wooden supports, with the back of the robe draping down.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the side profile of the head, the staff, and the book, with the robe's texture visible.
attribute_subject_matter: human figure holding a staff and book
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
